author: mark
text:
balls: document bare core repo as an alternative to the local pre-commit hook

The From-source section presented make-hooks as the way to enforce the 300-line and 100% coverage gates. It is one valid path, not the only one: a bare core repo with the worktree/merge model plus CI delivers the same guarantee, and which fits is circumstantial. Reframe the section as a recommendation and state the reasons to pick each -- the hook gives fast at-commit feedback but is per-clone, per-commit-costly, and --no-verify bypasses it; a bare core has no working tree so the working branch cannot be edited directly, making the bypass structurally impossible, at the cost of violations surfacing at review/CI rather than at the commit. Note that the two compose.
